synthesis_text: To reduce the risk of getting COVID-19, several measures are recommended. Practicing good personal hygiene, such as regular hand washing and covering coughs and sneezes, is important [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Maintaining physical or social distancing and avoiding close contact with infected individuals can help prevent transmission [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Using face masks or coverings in public settings is advised to reduce the spread of the virus [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Ensuring proper ventilation of indoor spaces decreases the risk of airborne transmission [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Staying up to date with COVID-19 vaccinations is crucial to reduce the risk of infection and severe outcomes [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006].

To reduce the risk of getting COVID-19, it is important to stay up to date on vaccinations, ensure good indoor airflow, practice physical distancing, wear masks in appropriate settings, and maintain good hygiene. Avoiding close contact with sick individuals and washing hands frequently are also effective measures. These strategies are supported by both local and external evidence [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001, doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023, doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006, doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016].

To reduce the risk of getting COVID-19, it is important to follow several preventive measures. These include avoiding crowded places and maintaining at least 1 metre distance from others [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016], wearing a mask in public places where physical distancing is not possible [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016], washing hands frequently with soap and water or an alcohol-based hand rub [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016], keeping indoor spaces well ventilated [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016], and getting vaccinated while continuing other preventive measures [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00012]. Additionally, staying up to date with COVID-19 vaccinations helps prevent serious illness and death [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001].
